ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Scott Hodges (Trustee).

The Court stated that it reviewed the accounting, that everything looks in order, and that the only issue is the request for fees which may result in a reduction that is sort of in line as to the services that were provided. Ms. Alavi stated that this is a very large and complex trust administration, that the full responsibility and liability fell on her Client, that California Rule of Court 7.776 allows the Court to take into consideration other factors, and that this case has a very long history and was highly contested. Ms. Alavi also stated that this involved an extreme case of hoarding, spoke of amounts of cash that were found in the rubble that had been chewed up by rodents, stated that Mr. Hodges has over 26 years of knowledge and experience, and that Mr. Hodges was able to get Ms. Tillman out of the house and cleaned the house. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that there are two large apartment complexes, that the tenants have taken advantage of Ms. Tillman, that the tenants pay $339.00 to $644.00 a month in rent, that the tenants are hostile and that the tenants are a huge liability to her Client. Furthermore, Ms. Alavi stated that Mr. Hodges has voluntarily reduced his fees for the property management fees, that during this accounting Ms. Tilman has died, that the sole beneficiary is an adult/a charity, that the charity has not expressed any objections and that the charity has consented. The Fifth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Terminate Continuing Court Supervision Of Trust; Vacating Prior Order Requiring Court Approval Prior To Sale Or Encumbrance Of Real Property; And To Convert Existing Bond To A Non-Court Supervised Bond And Setting Bond Amount
Off Calendar Jul-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by telephone for Scott Hodges (Trustee).

The Court stated that it read everything, that its tentative ruling is to deny the petition without prejudice as to terminating Court supervision, that the general practice is that the trust remain under Court supervision until there is an accounting up to the date of death and noted that the 5th accounting will cover the period of April 1, 2018 through December 5, 2019. Ms. Alavi stated that she was not aware of that, that the 5th accounting is not yet due and that her Client is working on it. Ms. Alavi also stated that the Settlor was an incapacitated Conservatee and needed supervision of the Court, that the Conservatee/Settlor is now deceased, that the sole Beneficiary (SPCA) has consented and is the only Party to take, and that the accounting will be provided to the SPCA. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that there are three parcels of real property that need to be sold, that the properties have been cleared out and are ready to be put on the market, and that with the covid pandemic, it could be damaging to the Beneficiary to wait for an accounting. Discussion was held as to language of the order with respect to Trustee being able to sell the properties. The Petition to Terminate Continuing Court Supervision of Trust, etc. is under submission. Status on filing of the 5th account is set for July 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Obtain Reverse Mortgage Secured By Trustors Residence And To Reduce The Trustees Bond
Off Calendar May-26-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges.

Ms. Alavi reported that historically Ms. Tillman had not been receiving rent, that the rents had been low, that the went through litigation to evict the elder abusers and retain new renters, that they had to pay for repairs prior to renting, that unit 6 on 6th Avenue is almost complete and will be bringing in $3,000.00 per month, that the 6th Avenue property currently has $50,000.00 in reserve but there is quite a bit that needs to be paid out and that the 16th Avenue property currently has $23,000.00 in reserve. Ms. Alavi further reported that the 6th Avenue property has 9 units bringing in $9586.00 per month (4 units at market rate, 5 units below market rate), the 16th Avenue property has 5 units bringing in $4850.00 per month (2 units at market rate and 3 units below market rate), that once unit 6 is rented the manager should be able to deposit $10,000.00 to $14,000.00 into the conservatorship, that there is deferred maintenance costs, that there is $72,000.00 in litigation fees owed and that the cost for the care of Ms. Tillman will increase in the next couple of months. The Court asked why we would not wait to see what the situation is with the variables before moving forward. Ms. Alavi stated that there are no guarantees and that they would never be able to pay the litigation counsel or the upgrades to the vacant units. The Petition for Authority to Obtain Reverse Mortgage Secured by Trustor's Residence, etc., was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Yeung. (Not Reported)
